message:
feat(tui,beads): enhance TUI rendering, theme, and widget styling; update beads tracking

- src/tui/render.rs: TUI render pipeline improvements
- src/tui/theme.rs: Theme styling refinements
- src/tui/widgets.rs: Widget rendering enhancements
- .beads/beads.db: Updated beads database state
- .beads/issues.jsonl: Updated issue tracking journal
- .beads/last-touched: Beads timestamp update

Skipped ephemeral: .claude/, SESSION_REPORT.md, SESSION_REPORT_EXPLORATION.md, test (binary)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1kn","title":"Project scaffolding: Cargo.toml, workspace structure, rust-toolchain.toml","description":"Set up the complete Rust project structure for storage_ballast_helper (sbh).

## Technical Approach
- Create Cargo.toml with edition = "2024", binary name = "sbh"
- Create rust-toolchain.toml targeting STABLE (edition 2024 is stable since Rust 1.85, Jan 2025)
- Set up directory structure: src/{main.rs, lib.rs, cli/, core/, monitor/, ballast/, scanner/, logger/, daemon/, platform/}
- Configure release profile for size optimization: opt-level="z", lto=true, codegen-units=1, panic="abort", strip=true
- Add #![forbid(unsafe_code)] to lib.rs
- Enable pedantic + nursery clippy lints

## Key Dependencies (initial)
- clap (derive) for CLI
- serde + serde_json for serialization
- rusqlite (bundled) for SQLite logging
- toml for configuration parsing
- thiserror for error derives
- colored for terminal output
- chrono for timestamps
- nix (Linux) / core-foundation (macOS) for platform-specific ops
- parking_lot for efficient locks
- crossbeam-channel for thread communication
- memchr for fast byte scanning
- regex for artifact pattern matching (bd-1sw)
- signal-hook for safe signal handling (bd-2s9)
- rand for ballast file random data generation (bd-25g)

## Design Rationale
Following asupersync's pattern: NO tokio dependency. Use std threads + crossbeam channels for the daemon monitoring loop. This keeps the binary small, startup fast, and avoids async runtime complexity for what is fundamentally a polling-based system daemon.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check` passes with zero warnings
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes
- Binary compiles to < 5MB release size
- Project structure mirrors the modular architecture described in the design","acceptance_criteria":"1. Cargo workspace and module layout compile cleanly with Rust 2024 toolchain and strict lint gates. 2. Baseline test harness is scaffolded: unit-test module structure plus e2e script entrypoint with verbose logging hooks. 3. Build profiles and forbid-unsafe policy are enforced in root configuration. 4. Core dependency set supports scanner/daemon/CLI architecture without placeholder shims. 5. Foundation docs describe how new modules register tests and structured logs.","notes":"Progress update: implemented crate-level unsafe forbids, baseline integration harness (tests/common + tests/integration_tests), executable scripts/e2e_test.sh with verbose case logs, and docs/testing-and-logging.md. Verified: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo test --test integration_tests, ./scripts/e2e_test.sh. Status remains in_progress until repository-wide clippy -D warnings debt (pre-existing across multiple modules) is resolved.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:43:36.253025401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:46:06.022861156Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:46:06.022841960Z","close_reason":"Complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"],"comments":[{"id":246,"issue_id":"bd-1kn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Fixed two issues: (1) Added missing dependencies: regex (for bd-1sw pattern matching), signal-hook (for bd-2s9 signal handling), rand (for bd-25g ballast random data). These were referenced by downstream beads but not listed in the scaffolding bead's dep list. (2) Changed 'targeting nightly' to 'targeting STABLE' — edition 2024 has been stable since Rust 1.85 (Jan 2025). No reason to use nightly.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:12:35Z"},{"id":247,"issue_id":"bd-1kn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"=== BEAD REVIEW SESSION COMPLETE (2026-02-14) ===

Summary of all revisions made across the 36-bead graph:

STRUCTURAL FIX:
- Removed bd-x9z → bd-2pj dependency (scoring engine is pure computation, shouldn't depend on fs stats collector)

BEAD ENHANCEMENTS (via comments with implementation guidance):
- bd-1kn: Added missing deps (regex, signal-hook, rand). Fixed edition 2024 → stable channel
- bd-x9z: Full description rewrite. Established pure-computation contract (zero I/O). Added ScoringInput struct with explicit now timestamp and is_open flag for determinism
- bd-1w9: Walker must collect structural_markers during traversal for scoring engine
- bd-25g: Added fallocate() optimization for ext4/xfs (instant vs 25-50s). Added file locking for concurrent daemon/CLI access
- bd-48o: Added bounded channel sizing, backpressure strategy, thread panic recovery (3 strikes in 5 min → stop respawning)
- bd-7ls: Clarified role: cross-cutting test infrastructure + coverage verification + gap-filling (not duplicate of per-module tests)
- bd-1sw: Added error handling for invalid custom regex patterns from config
- bd-394: Strengthened 4-level fallback chain (file → fallback_path → stderr → discard). write_all() for atomic lines
- bd-2q9: Added 8 more E2E test cases (signals, concurrency, determinism, perf)
- bd-2s9: Noted signal-hook crate dependency in Cargo.toml
- bd-1hh: Added /proc/*/fd open-file check performance analysis and caching strategy
- bd-3qw: Added PID controller config section and special locations config section
- bd-2np: Defined state.json format, atomic writes, staleness detection
- bd-224: Added cross-mount ballast edge case (ballast must be on same FS as pressure)
- bd-1gm: Added try_send() non-blocking logging design, dedicated logger thread owns SQLite
- bd-26g: Fixed ReadWritePaths conflict with ProtectHome=read-only
- bd-2f8: Added startup check that SQLite db is NOT on monitored filesystem

GRAPH HEALTH (post-review):
- 36 nodes, 64 edges, 0 cycles
- Critical path length: 11 (unchanged)
- 1 ready bead (bd-1kn)
- 4 articulation points
- bd-48o remains the key bottleneck (unavoidable — it's the integration point)","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:16:49Z"},{"id":248,"issue_id":"bd-1kn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Additional dependency needed for TUI dashboard (bd-3s5): crossterm crate for terminal manipulation. This is NOT needed in initial scaffolding — add crossterm as an optional dependency gated behind a 'tui' feature flag: [features] tui = ['dep:crossterm', 'dep:unicode-width']. This way the core binary stays small and the dashboard is opt-in at compile time.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:53:56Z"},{"id":249,"issue_id":"bd-1kn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MINOR: Time handling convention — use std::time::SystemTime and std::time::Duration throughout the codebase. chrono is in the dependency list but should ONLY be used for user-facing formatting (display, log timestamps, JSON serialization). All internal calculations, comparisons, and storage should use SystemTime/Duration. This avoids chrono's heavier API surface for simple elapsed-time checks.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:54:41Z"},{"id":250,"issue_id":"bd-1kn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW-2: Additional dependency: toml_edit crate for config file modification preserving formatting/comments. Required by bd-7vl (sbh tune --apply). Add as optional dependency behind a feature flag if desired.","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:03:22Z"}]}

Validation: rch exec "cargo test --lib scoring" PASS; rch exec "cargo check --all-targets" PASS; cargo fmt --check PASS. rch exec "cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings" still fails with broad pre-existing lint debt outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-02-15T06:42:25Z"},{"id":252,"issue_id":"bd-1q3","author":"WildBeacon","text":"Follow-up refinement in src/scanner/scoring.rs: added uncertainty_adjusted_losses(), routed decision expected losses through uncertainty-aware adjustment, widened review band and posterior thresholding under uncertainty, expanded evidence ledger with base-vs-adjusted losses + calibration/uncertainty terms, and added unit test uncertainty_adjustment_penalizes_delete_loss_more_than_keep_loss. Validation: cargo fmt --check PASS; rch exec 'cargo test --lib scoring' PASS (12); rch exec 'cargo check --all-targets' PASS; rch exec 'cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings' blocked by pre-existing unrelated lint in src/monitor/fs_stats.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-15T06:50:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1sw","title":"Build artifact pattern registry with heuristic classification rules","description":"## Deliverable
A comprehensive, extensible registry of filename/path patterns that identify build artifacts, temporary files, and cache directories across all major build systems.

## Technical Approach
### Pattern Categories (from real-world agent swarm data)

#### Rust/Cargo Patterns (highest priority - this is the primary use case)
Matching patterns observed across real agent sessions:
- `target/` - standard Cargo target directory
- `.target*` - hidden target variants (.target_boldmeadow_auth3, .target-topaz, .target-opus)
- `_target_*` - underscore-prefixed (_target_opus_main, _target_codex_deepdive)
- `.tmp_target*` - temp targets (.tmp_target_codex_or, .tmp_target_pearlgorge)
- `cargo-target-*` - named cargo targets (cargo-target-quietwillow-mvcc, cargo-target-pearl)
- `cargo_*` - alternate cargo patterns (cargo_opus_prime)
- `*-target` - suffix targets (jadefinch-target, scarletwaterfall-target)
- `target-*` - prefix targets (target-ubuntu-am, target-maroon)
- `.tmp_cargo_home_*` - temp cargo homes
- `.tmp-codex-*` - codex temp dirs
- `.tmp-pijs-*` - pi_agent temp dirs
- `.tmp-ext-*` - extension test dirs
- `pi_agent_*` - pi_agent variants (pi_agent_cyanrobin, pi_agent_rust_opus)
- `pi_target_*` - pi_target variants
- `pi_opus_*` - pi_opus variants
- `.tmp-opt-target*` - optimization test targets
- `br-build*` - beads_rust build dirs
- `cass-target*` - cass build dirs

**Structural indicators within directories:**
- Contains `incremental/` → almost certainly a Rust target dir
- Contains `deps/` + `build/` → very likely Rust target
- Contains `.fingerprint/` → definitely Rust target
- Contains `release/` or `debug/` with .d files → Rust target

#### Node.js Patterns
- `node_modules/`
- `.next/` (Next.js build)
- `dist/`
- `.cache/` (various build tools)
- `.parcel-cache/`

#### Python Patterns
- `__pycache__/`
- `.venv/` / `venv/`
- `*.egg-info/`
- `.mypy_cache/`
- `.pytest_cache/`

#### General Build Patterns
- `build/` (many languages)
- `.build/`
- `out/`
- `.gradle/`
- `.tox/`

#### Cache Patterns
- `~/.cache/go-build`
- `~/.cache/pip`
- `~/.cache/huggingface`
- `~/.cache/actcache`

### Pattern Registry Implementation
```rust
pub struct ArtifactPattern {
    pub name: &'static str,
    pub description: &'static str,
    pub name_regex: Regex,
    pub structural_markers: Vec<&'static str>,  // subdirs that confirm classification
    pub confidence: f64,         // 0.0-1.0 base confidence
    pub category: ArtifactCategory,
    pub typical_size_range: (u64, u64),  // expected size range for plausibility
}

pub enum ArtifactCategory {
    RustTarget,     // highest confidence
    NodeModules,
    PythonCache,
    BuildOutput,
    CacheDir,
    TempDir,
    AgentWorkspace, // agent-specific temp builds
    Unknown,
}
```

### Matching Algorithm
For each discovered directory:
1. Check name against all patterns (regex match)
2. If name matches, check structural markers (does it contain incremental/, deps/, etc.)
3. Combine name confidence with structural confidence
4. Return ArtifactClassification with overall confidence

```rust
pub struct ArtifactClassification {
    pub pattern_name: &'static str,
    pub category: ArtifactCategory,
    pub name_confidence: f64,
    pub structural_confidence: f64,
    pub combined_confidence: f64,
}
```

### Extensibility
Users can add custom patterns via config:
```toml
[[scanner.custom_patterns]]
name = "my-tool-cache"
regex = "^\\.my-tool-cache$"
confidence = 0.8
category = "CacheDir"
```

## Design Rationale
The pattern list is derived from REAL data collected from weeks of agent swarm operation. The naming conventions are diverse and creative (agents name targets after themselves: boldmeadow, quietwillow, etc.), so we need comprehensive regex patterns. Structural markers add a second layer of verification: even if a directory is named "target", we check if it LOOKS like a Rust target dir inside. This prevents false positives.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All observed agent naming patterns from the specification are covered
- Structural markers correctly identify Rust target directories
- No false positives on common directory names (e.g., a user's "target" document folder)
- Custom patterns loadable from config
- Confidence scores reflect actual classification quality
- Unit tests for every pattern category
- Unit test: directory named "target" but containing only documents → low confidence
- Unit test: directory named ".target_opus_main" with incremental/ inside → high confidence","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic pattern classification for identical inputs across all built-in and custom rules. 2. Unit tests cover positive/negative fixtures, precedence, and confidence calibration boundaries. 3. Integration tests validate handoff into walker/scorer without false category drift. 4. E2E scenarios exercise real artifact trees and verify expected matched categories. 5. Detailed match-trace logging includes pattern id, confidence, and suppression/veto reasons.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:47:37.220914613Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:46:14.652852740Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:46:14.652831130Z","close_reason":"Implemented in scanner/patterns.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["scanner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sw","depends_on_id":"bd-1kn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T00:06:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1sw","depends_on_id":"bd-3uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T00:06:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":253,"issue_id":"bd-1sw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Missing error handling for user-supplied custom regex patterns:

When a user adds a custom pattern in config:
  [[scanner.custom_patterns]]
  name = "my-cache"
  regex = "[invalid(regex"  # <-- broken regex

The pattern registry must:
1. Validate each custom regex at config load time using Regex::new()
2. If invalid: emit SBH-4001 error with the regex string, position of syntax error, and suggestion
3. NEVER crash — skip the invalid pattern and log a warning
4. In strict mode (sbh config validate): report all invalid patterns as errors
5. In normal operation: silently skip invalid patterns but log them to JSONL

This follows the "don't let user config mistakes crash the daemon" principle. A typo in one custom pattern should not prevent sbh from monitoring with all its built-in patterns.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:13:31Z"}]}
@@ -97,6 +98,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-394","title":"JSONL append-only structured logger","description":"## Deliverable
Append-only JSONL (JSON Lines) writer that provides a second, independent log of all sbh activity, optimized for agent consumption and grep-ability.

## Technical Approach
### JSONL Format
Each line is a self-contained JSON object:
```json
{"ts":"2026-02-14T16:30:00Z","event":"artifact_delete","path":"/data/projects/foo/.target_opus","size":3456789012,"score":0.87,"factors":{"location":0.85,"name":0.90,"age":0.95,"size":0.80,"structure":0.85},"pressure":"orange","free_pct":8.3,"duration_ms":145,"ok":true}
{"ts":"2026-02-14T16:30:01Z","event":"ballast_release","path":"/var/lib/sbh/ballast/SBH_BALLAST_FILE_00010.dat","size":1073741824,"pressure":"red","free_pct":4.2,"ok":true}
{"ts":"2026-02-14T16:30:02Z","event":"pressure_change","level":"red->orange","free_pct":12.1,"rate_bps":-50000000}
```

### Writer Implementation
```rust
pub struct JsonlWriter {
    file: BufWriter<File>,
    path: PathBuf,
    bytes_written: u64,
    max_size: u64,           // rotation threshold
    rotation_count: u32,
}
```

### Write Strategy
- Buffer writes in memory (BufWriter with 64KB buffer)
- Flush after every logical event (not every line - batch related events)
- fsync every 10 seconds or on pressure change (not every write - too expensive)
- Each line is atomic: write the full line then newline in one write() call

### Rotation
When file exceeds max_size_mb:
- Rename current file to sbh.jsonl.1 (shift existing rotations)
- Open new sbh.jsonl
- Keep up to 5 rotated files (configurable)

### Agent-Friendly Design
Agents can:
- `tail -f /var/lib/sbh/sbh.jsonl` for live monitoring
- `grep "artifact_delete" sbh.jsonl | jq .` for filtering
- `jq -s '[.[] | select(.event == "artifact_delete")] | length' sbh.jsonl` for counts
- Parse each line independently (no multi-line records)

### Why Both SQLite AND JSONL?
1. SQLite: efficient queries, aggregation, statistics, joins
2. JSONL: portable, grep-able, agent-friendly, survives SQLite corruption
3. JSONL works even when disk is critically full (can be on a different filesystem)
4. JSONL is the "black box recorder" - if everything else fails, this survives

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each line is valid JSON
- Lines are atomic (no partial writes visible to readers)
- Rotation works correctly without data loss
- Agent can tail -f and get real-time events
- All event types have consistent field names
- Timestamps in ISO 8601 UTC
- File rotation handles edge cases (rename failures, etc.)
- Unit tests for writing, rotation, and parsing","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:49:33.020087320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:51:08.884116886Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:51:08.884093723Z","close_reason":"JSONL logger implemented in src/logger/jsonl.rs: LogEntry/EventType/Severity types, JsonlWriter with BufWriter (64KB), atomic write_all lines, file rotation with configurable max_size/rotated_files, 4-level fallback chain (file→fallback→stderr→discard), fsync batching, try_recover, 6 passing tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logger"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-394","depends_on_id":"bd-3qw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T00:06:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-394","depends_on_id":"bd-3uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T00:06:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":326,"issue_id":"bd-394","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Strengthened fallback chain specification:

The JSONL writer must implement this explicit degradation ladder:

1. NORMAL: BufWriter → file (64KB buffer, fsync every 10s)
2. DEGRADED-1: If file write fails (disk full), try reopening file on a DIFFERENT filesystem. Config should allow jsonl_fallback_path (e.g., /dev/shm/sbh.jsonl for RAM-backed fallback)
3. DEGRADED-2: If fallback path also fails, write to stderr with [SBH-JSONL] prefix so journald/syslog captures events
4. DEGRADED-3: If stderr write fails (shouldn't happen but defensive), discard events silently — the daemon MUST NOT crash for logging failures

Each degradation level transition must be logged at the NEXT available level.

Also add: Write JSONL lines using write_all() for a complete buffer (line + newline assembled first), NOT separate write() calls for line and newline. This prevents interleaved partial lines when the file is being tailed by another process.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:13:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-395","title":"Agent/process disk usage attribution (sbh blame)","description":"## Deliverable
Track and display which running processes or agents are consuming the most disk space in build artifacts. sbh status --blame and sbh blame commands.

## Usage
  sbh blame                    # Show disk usage by agent/process
  sbh blame --json             # Machine-readable output

## Output
```
Disk Usage by Agent/Process:

  Agent/Process          │ Build Dirs │ Total Size │ Oldest   │ Newest
  claude-code (PID 1234) │    23      │   45.2 GB  │ 12h ago  │ 15m ago
  codex-cli (PID 5678)   │     8      │   12.1 GB  │  4h ago  │ 30m ago
  pi-agent (PID 9012)    │    14      │   28.7 GB  │  8h ago  │  1h ago
  (orphaned)             │    31      │   67.4 GB  │  3d ago  │  6h ago

  Total: 76 build dirs, 153.4 GB
  Orphaned dirs (no running process) are the safest to clean.
```

## Implementation
1. Scan /proc/*/cwd to find which processes have CWD in project directories
2. Correlate discovered artifacts with the project directory of running processes
3. "Orphaned" artifacts belong to projects where no process currently has CWD
4. Use process binary name (from /proc/*/comm) as agent identifier
5. Cache /proc scan results (refreshed every 30s or on demand)

## Design Rationale
Helps users understand which agents are the worst disk offenders. "Orphaned" artifacts (no running process) are the safest to clean and can be prioritized. This is a diagnostic tool that makes sbh's decisions more transparent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correctly attributes artifacts to running processes
- Orphaned artifacts identified and highlighted
- Works when many agents run simultaneously
- Performance: <2s even with 100+ processes and 10K+ artifacts
- JSON output for agent consumption
- Handles /proc access gracefully (permission denied on some PIDs)","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests cover PID-to-path attribution, orphan detection, and aggregation correctness. 2. Integration tests validate /proc scanning behavior with permission denials and stale process state. 3. E2E scenarios validate blame output across concurrent agent workloads and orphan cleanup opportunities. 4. JSON output schema remains stable for agent consumption. 5.  {"id":"bd-39b","title":"Rewrite AGENTS.md for storage_ballast_helper project","description":"## Deliverable
Replace the current dcg-focused AGENTS.md with one tailored for the storage_ballast_helper (sbh) project.

## Technical Approach
### Structure (following dcg's excellent pattern)
1. Rule 0: Fundamental override prerogative
2. Rule 1: No file deletion without permission
3. Git branch conventions
4. Toolchain section (Rust, Cargo, edition, dependencies)
5. Code editing discipline
6. Compiler checks (cargo check, clippy, fmt)
7. Testing section (unit tests, integration, E2E, stress)
8. CI/CD pipeline overview
9. Release process
10. Architecture overview:
    - Module diagram
    - Key files table
    - Data flow description
11. Configuration reference
12. CLI quick reference
13. Scoring engine documentation
14. Beads/bv workflow integration (keep existing sections)
15. Session completion protocol

### Key Differences from dcg's AGENTS.md
- Architecture section describes sbh's monitoring/scanning/cleanup pipeline
- No "hook protocol" section (sbh is a standalone daemon, not a hook)
- Add scoring engine documentation
- Add ballast system documentation
- Add pressure levels and PID controller overview
- Add build artifact pattern reference

## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive and self-contained (agent can work from AGENTS.md alone)
- All key files documented
- CLI commands documented
- Architecture clearly explained
- Testing instructions complete
- No references to dcg left","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:55:04.827031477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:18:49.279774512Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T00:18:49.279705413Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39b","depends_on_id":"bd-1kn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T00:06:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":328,"issue_id":"bd-39b","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MINOR: AGENTS.md rewrite depends only on bd-2rq (CLI framework) to know the final command structure. Current dep (bd-2rq) is sufficient.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:54:47Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3cj","title":"Statistics and metrics query engine with time-window aggregation","description":"## Deliverable
Query engine that provides statistics about sbh's recent behavior across multiple time windows, enabling agents and users to understand what sbh has been doing.

## Technical Approach
### Time Windows
Report statistics for: 10 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 6 hours, 24 hours, 3 days, 7 days

### Statistics Available
```rust
pub struct WindowStats {
    pub window: Duration,
    pub deletions: DeletionStats,
    pub ballast: BallastStats,
    pub pressure: PressureStats,
}

pub struct DeletionStats {
    pub count: u64,                      // number of items deleted
    pub total_bytes_freed: u64,          // total bytes freed
    pub avg_size: u64,                   // average size of deleted items
    pub median_size: u64,
    pub largest_deletion: Option<PathInfo>,
    pub most_common_category: String,    // e.g., "RustTarget"
    pub most_common_pattern: String,     // e.g., "cargo-target-*"
    pub avg_score: f64,                  // average candidacy score
    pub avg_age_hours: f64,              // average age at time of deletion
    pub failures: u64,                   // failed deletions
}

pub struct BallastStats {
    pub files_released: u64,
    pub files_replenished: u64,
    pub current_inventory: u64,
    pub bytes_available: u64,
}

pub struct PressureStats {
    pub time_in_green_pct: f64,
    pub time_in_yellow_pct: f64,
    pub time_in_orange_pct: f64,
    pub time_in_red_pct: f64,
    pub time_in_critical_pct: f64,
    pub transitions: u64,                // number of pressure level changes
    pub worst_level_reached: PressureLevel,
    pub current_level: PressureLevel,
    pub current_free_pct: f64,
}
```

### Query Implementation
```rust
pub struct StatsEngine {
    sqlite: Arc<SqliteLogger>,
}

impl StatsEngine {
    /// Get stats for all standard windows
    pub fn summary(&self) -> Result<Vec<WindowStats>, SbhError>;
    
    /// Get stats for a specific window
    pub fn window_stats(&self, window: Duration) -> Result<WindowStats, SbhError>;
    
    /// Get top-N most deleted patterns
    pub fn top_patterns(&self, n: usize, window: Duration) -> Result<Vec<PatternStat>, SbhError>;
    
    /// Get top-N largest deletions
    pub fn top_deletions(&self, n: usize, window: Duration) -> Result<Vec<DeletionDetail>, SbhError>;
    
    /// Export stats as JSON (for agent consumption)
    pub fn export_json(&self) -> Result<serde_json::Value, SbhError>;
}
```

### SQL Queries
Efficient aggregation using SQLite's date functions:
```sql
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) as count,
    SUM(size_bytes) as total_bytes,
    AVG(size_bytes) as avg_size,
    AVG(score) as avg_score
FROM activity_log
WHERE event_type = 'artifact_delete'
  AND timestamp > datetime('now', '-1 hour')
  AND success = 1;
```

### Output Format
Both human-readable (colored table for terminal) and JSON (for agents):
```
sbh stats
┌─────────┬────────┬──────────────┬──────────────┬────────────┐
│ Window  │ Deleted│ Space Freed  │ Avg Size     │ Avg Score  │
├─────────┼────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────────┤
│ 10 min  │     3  │       4.2 GB │       1.4 GB │      0.87  │
│ 30 min  │    12  │      18.7 GB │       1.6 GB │      0.82  │
│  1 hour │    28  │      42.1 GB │       1.5 GB │      0.79  │
│  6 hours│    85  │     124.3 GB │       1.5 GB │      0.76  │
│ 24 hours│   312  │     467.8 GB │       1.5 GB │      0.74  │
│  3 days │   891  │    1284.2 GB │       1.4 GB │      0.73  │
│  7 days │  2104  │    3012.7 GB │       1.4 GB │      0.72  │
└─────────┴────────┴──────────────┴──────────────┴────────────┘

Most deleted patterns: cargo-target-* (42%), .target_* (28%), target/ (18%)
Current pressure: GREEN (23.4% free)
Ballast: 8/10 files available (8 GB reclaimable)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All time windows produce correct statistics
- Statistics match actual activity log data (verified by cross-checking)
- JSON output is well-formed and complete
- Human-readable output is clear and informative
- Queries are efficient (< 100ms even with 100K+ log entries)
- Most common patterns correctly computed
- Handles empty database gracefully
- Unit tests with pre-populated test data","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:50:15.096388928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:59:45.809126758Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:59:45.809098235Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logger"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cj","depends_on_id":"bd-1gm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T00:06:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":329,"issue_id":"bd-3cj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MINOR: Stats engine schema coupling with SQLite logger (bd-2f8): The stats engine queries the SQLite database directly. The schema is defined in bd-2f8 and the stats engine reads it. This is intentional tight coupling — they share the same database. Any schema changes in bd-2f8 must be reflected in bd-3cj queries. Document the schema contract in both beads.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:54:53Z"}]}
